articles.title: Trump says Iran shot down US Apache helicopter over Strait of Hormuz, vows response
articles.summary: President Trump said the US 'must, of necessity, respond' after an Iranian drone brought down a US Army Apache helicopter patrolling over the Strait of Hormuz late on June 8. Both crew members were rescued about two hours later and were unharmed. Trump did not specify the nature or timing of any retaliation. Iran has not publicly claimed responsibility; Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said US forces were putting themselves at risk in the region but did not mention an Iranian role. The incident threatens the halt in Israel-Iran strikes announced hours earlier.

articles.title: Iran, Israel say attacks halted as Trump claims peace talks in 'final throes'
articles.summary: Iran and Israel both said they had stopped fresh strikes, with Prime Minister Netanyahu stopping short of acknowledging a formal ceasefire that Trump said the two sides were aiming for. Iran suspended operations but warned it would resume them if Israeli attacks on southern Lebanon continue, insisting any US deal must also cover Lebanon. Trump said negotiators were in the 'final throes' of a Middle East peace deal; a senior Iranian official said Tehran had 'no problem' continuing talks provided Washington was sincere.

articles.title: Israel kills nine in Tyre as Lebanon war reaches 100 days
articles.summary: Israeli forces killed at least nine people in the southern city of Tyre after issuing forced displacement orders, continuing deadly strikes on southern Lebanon. June 9 marked 100 days since the start of Israel's latest military intensification against Lebanon. Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health says more than 3,600 people have been killed over that period, including at least 245 children, with dozens of villages destroyed. Israel and Lebanon have held direct talks for the first time in decades, but ceasefire implementation has repeatedly stalled.

articles.title: Israel will keep operating against Hezbollah, strike Beirut if attacked, Katz says
articles.summary: Defense Minister Israel Katz said the military would continue operating against Hezbollah in Lebanon and would strike Beirut if the group attacks Israel, framing the Lebanon campaign as separate from any Iran ceasefire. The IDF warned Tyre residents to flee ahead of strikes, a day after Iran cautioned against Israeli 'aggression' in Lebanon. The IDF also said it had completed an initial probe into an infiltration attack by a gunman on the Lebanese border but could not yet determine his affiliation.

articles.title: Hamas reports progress in Cairo talks on Gaza ceasefire and roadmap
articles.summary: Hamas said progress had been made in Cairo talks with Palestinian factions and Egyptian, Qatari and Turkish mediators on implementing a roadmap to advance the fragile Gaza ceasefire and Trump's plan. Hamas signalled it would hand governance to a technocratic national committee and keep weapons off the streets but would not surrender its arms outright, tying any decommissioning to an Israeli withdrawal. Israel and the Trump-appointed Board of Peace envoy have conditioned the next phase on Hamas disarmament. The second phase also covers Israeli withdrawal, reconstruction and a transitional governing body.

articles.title: Six nations sanction networks financing West Bank settler attacks
articles.summary: The UK, Australia, Canada, France, New Zealand and Norway announced coordinated sanctions targeting networks 'financing and enabling' settler attacks against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, citing record settlement expansion and rising violence. Amnesty International criticised the UK measures as leaving the 'architects' of settler violence untouched. The move follows recent settler attacks, including an assault on the town of Huwara that wounded several residents.

articles.title: Israeli authorities seized 192 acres of Palestinian land in a month
articles.summary: Israeli authorities took control of about 192 acres of private and municipal Palestinian land in the occupied West Bank during May for security, military and settlement purposes. About 97 acres of private land near Jenin and Qabatiya were taken for military sites, while roughly 95 acres of municipal land belonging to Al-Bireh and Beitin, near Ramallah, were seized to build a road serving the Givat Assaf outpost.

articles.title: Israeli strike on Jabalia refugee camp kills three, including 8-year-old boy
articles.summary: Palestinian media affiliated with Hamas reported that three people were killed in an Israeli strike on the Jabalia refugee camp in northern Gaza, among them an eight-year-old boy, Jad Suleiman. The strike came as Israel continued to press deeper into Gaza and mediators convened in Cairo to try to salvage the ceasefire.

articles.title: Israeli army demolishes six Palestinian homes in occupied West Bank
articles.summary: The Israeli army demolished six Palestinian-owned homes and a carpentry workshop across the occupied West Bank, displacing dozens of people in the latest round of demolitions. Rights groups say such operations have accelerated alongside settlement expansion.

articles.title: Saudi GDP rises 3% in Q1 as non-oil sector drives expansion
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia's GDP grew 3% year-on-year in the first quarter, led by the non-oil sector, according to official data. Riyadh also awarded a $533m Jubail Port contract as part of a wider logistics push, qualified 24 firms for new mining exploration licences, and reported capital-market assets up 18% to more than $320bn in 2025, underscoring the kingdom's diversification drive amid regional tensions.

articles.title: Iran's World Cup squad lands in Mexico after securing US visas
articles.summary: Iran's national football team players and support staff obtained US visas and arrived in Mexico to begin preparations for the World Cup, a rare non-conflict development involving Iran amid the ongoing war and diplomatic standoff with Washington.
